title: Second harmonic generation and third harmonic generation in topological insulator-based van der Waals metamaterials
authors: Alessandra Di Gaspare, Sara Ghayeb, Craig Knox, Edmund H. Linfield, Joshua Freeman, Miriam S. Vitiello
abstract: High-order harmonic generation (HHG) in solids - the frequency up-conversion of an optical signal - is governed by symmetries. At terahertz (THz) frequencies, HHG is a key technology to access high frequency spectral windows that are usually difficult to cover using conventional solid state laser technologies. This effect has been recently exploited in graphene where HHG has been demonstrated, albeit only at odd multiples of the driving frequency owing to its inherent centro-symmetry. In topological insulators (TIs), the combination of spin-orbit interaction and time-reversal symmetry create an insulating bulk state with an inverted band order, inseparably connected with conducting surface states. TIs have been predicted to support unconventional high harmonic generation from the bulk and topological surface, which are usually difficult to be distinguished. However, no experimental results have been provided, so far. Here, we exploit the strong optical field amplification provided by an array of single or double split ring resonators, with embedded Bi2Se3 or (InxBi(1-x))2Se3/ Bi2Se3 van der Waals heterostructures, to achieve up-conversion in the 6.4 (even) - 9.7 (odd) THz frequency range. This results from bulk centro-symmetry (odd states) and symmetry breaking in the topological surface states (odd and even).
summary: The researchers experimentally demonstrated second and third harmonic generation in topological insulator-based metamaterials at THz frequencies. By using split ring resonators to amplify the optical field, they were able to observe even and odd harmonics arising from the distinct symmetries of the bulk and surface states. This provides a way to experimentally distinguish between different electronic contributions in topological materials.
main_problem: The difficulty in experimentally distinguishing between bulk and topological surface state contributions to high-order harmonic generation (HHG) in topological insulators.
main_result: Demonstration of both second (even) and third (odd) harmonic generation in the 6.4 - 9.7 THz range, enabled by symmetry breaking in surface states and bulk centrosymmetry.
method: Using an array of single or double split ring resonators to provide strong optical field amplification for THz-frequency up-conversion.
model_or_system: Van der Waals heterostructures consisting of Bi2Se3 or (InxBi(1-x))2Se3/Bi2Se3 embedded within split ring resonator metamaterials.
key_observables: Second harmonic generation (even THz frequencies) and third harmonic generation (odd THz frequencies).
important_parameters: THz frequency range of 6.4 to 9.7 THz; presence of spin-orbit interaction and time-reversal symmetry.
paper_structure: The paper introduces the concept of HHG in solids, discusses the symmetry properties of graphene and topological insulators, and presents experimental results using metamaterial-enhanced THz up-conversion.
